data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/21/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Rise after S&P 500, Dow's Largest Drop Since October
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher early Wednesday after the S&P 500 and the 30-stock Dow recorded their largest single-day drops since October 10. Traders are reacting to President Donald Trump's threat to escalate tariffs over his demand to acquire Greenland.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.33%, 0.34%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 3:20 a.m. EST on January 21.

During Tuesday's regular trading session, the three major indexes closed sharply lower, with technology stocks leading the selloff. The Dow declined by 1.76%, the S&P 500 fell by 2.06%, and the Nasdaq Composite dropped 2.39%.

After the markets closed, Netflix (NFLX) stock fell more than 5%, despite beating sales and earnings expectations for Q4FY25 and reporting a solid surge in global paid subscribers to 325 million. Moreover, the streaming giant revised its bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) to an all-cash offer, fending off competition from Paramount Skydance (PSKY).

Looking forward, traders are focused on Trump's speech at the World Economic Forum in Davos today, where he will discuss the Greenland issue. Additionally, economic reports on housing starts, MBA mortgage applications, building permits, and pending home sales will be released today.

On the earnings front, companies reporting today include Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Halliburton (HAL), and Charles Schwab (SCHW).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down, floating near 4.26%. WTI crude oil futures were trending lower, hovering near $59.63 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $4,855 per ounce on Wednesday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower today as traders anticipate Trump's speech in front of world leaders at Davos.

Asia-Pacific Markets Mixed

Asia-Pacific markets traded mixed on January 21 amid escalated geopolitical tensions involving Iran and Greenland.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index rose 0.36%. In China, the Shanghai Composite slipped 0.08%, while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.80%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei dropped 0.41%, and the Topix declined 0.99%.

data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Intel (INTC) Is About to Report Q4 Earnings Tomorrow. Here's What to Expect
data.description:
Chip giant Intel (INTC) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth-quarter 2025 after the market closes on Thursday, January 22. INTC stock has risen over 123% in 2025, driven by major deals, including a partnership with Nvidia (NVDA), strong support from the U.S. government, and progress on its 18A chip technology. However, Intel's foundry business remains a concern, due to production delays and uncertainty over its next-generation chip technology. Meanwhile, Wall Street analysts expect Intel to report earnings per share of $0.08, down 38.5% from the year-ago quarter.

Revenues are expected to decrease by 6% from the year-ago quarter to $13.40 billion, according to data from the TipRanks Forecast page.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Huang Seeks to Revive China Business with Upcoming Visit
data.description:
Nvidia's (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang is planning a trip to China in late January to reopen this key market for the semiconductor company's artificial intelligence (AI) chips, Bloomberg reported. Huang's China visit, which he generally makes around the Lunar New Year holidays, comes at a crucial time, given the uncertainty around Nvidia's chip exports.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

NVDA CEO Eyes Growth in China

Nvidia's CEO will be attending company parties and is also expected to visit Beijing, though it is still not clear if he will meet senior Chinese officials.

The Trump administration recently eased export restrictions by allowing Nvidia to sell its H200 chips to China in return for a 25% cut on such sales. Washington has also imposed additional conditions on H200 exports, including an assurance that production of these chips won't affect the capacity to manufacture chips for the domestic market.

Despite the Trump administration's approval, Nvidia's exports to China remain uncertain, given the reluctance of Chinese authorities amid ongoing trade tensions with the U.S. Following the Trump administration's approval, Reuters reported that Chinese customs authorities told customs agents not to permit H200 AI chips to enter the country.

Additionally, citing sources, Bloomberg reported that China will prohibit the use of H200 chips by the military, sensitive government agencies, critical infrastructure, and state-owned enterprises due to security reasons. Moreover, China is encouraging the domestic manufacturing of AI chips to reduce dependence on U.S. tech giants.

Is NVDA Stock a Good Buy Now?

Despite uncertainty around China sales, Wall Street remains bullish on Nvidia stock, driven by solid demand for the company's AI GPUs (graphics processing units) and continued innovation, as reflected in the Blackwell and Rubin platforms.

The Strong Buy consensus rating on Nvidia stock is based on 39 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell recommendation. The average NVDA stock price target of $263.44 indicates about 48% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Cathie Wood Buys the Dip in Broadcom and BYD Shares, Dumps Kratos Defense
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio moves on Tuesday, January 20, as revealed in daily fund disclosures. These trades underscore ARK's continued optimism about the electric vehicle (EV) and semiconductor sectors, despite chip shortages and softening auto demand.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Broadcom Stock (AVGO) – Wood purchased 32,408 shares of chipmaker Broadcom through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) for about $10.8 million. AVGO shares fell 5.4% yesterday, amid a Chinese regulator's directive to stop using foreign software solutions on national security grounds. Broadcom's VMware software was among the list of 12 U.S. and Israeli software vendors targeted on January 15.

BYD Co. Stock (BYDDF) – Wood also continued accumulating shares of Chinese EV leader BYD, adding 205,748 shares worth $2.53 million through the ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ). BYD surpassed auto sales of American EV giant Tesla (TSLA), crushing its global dominance. BYDDF shares dipped 2.2% on January 20. This move follows ARK's sale of $38 million in Tesla stock last week, signaling a pivot toward other EV players.

WeRide Stock (WRD) – Wood has shown a strong interest in autonomous vehicle (AV) technology. The ARKQ ETF purchased 577,099 shares of Chinese ride-hailing service platform WeRide, totaling $4.9 million. The robotaxi company has been expanding its fleet and operations in Beijing and Guangzhou.

Kratos Defense (KTOS) – The largest trade of the day was the sale of 171,871 shares of Kratos Defense & Security Solutions, worth $22.12 million. The sale was made through the ARKK, ARKQ, and ARKX funds. Wood's move comes as a surprise since defense stocks have been in the news due to the ongoing geopolitical tensions involving Iran and Greenland.

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool:

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on all four stocks, with WRD stock offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Rise after S&P 500 Sees Steep One-Day Losses Ahead of Trump's Davos Speech
data.description:
U.S. stock futures rose ahead of Wednesday's session after the market suffered its worst day in three months. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) Index were up 0.39%, 0.18%, and 0.36%, respectively, at 1:21 a.m. EDT on January 21.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stocks fell sharply in Tuesday's session after President Donald Trump renewed tariff threats tied to Greenland, just ahead of his scheduled address at the World Economic Forum in Davos on Wednesday. The Dow dropped about 1.8%, while the S&P 500 slid roughly 2.1%. Technology stocks led the selloff, pushing the Nasdaq Composite down 2.4%. All three major indexes posted their worst daily losses since October 10 and turned negative for 2026.

Tensions escalated further after Trump threatened to impose 200% tariffs on French wine and Champagne, following reports that Emmanuel Macron declined a role on Trump's proposed Gaza "Board of Peace." The developments have added to investor uncertainty as global leaders gather in Davos.

Investors are now turning their focus to earnings. Netflix (NFLX) shares fell in after-hours trading despite a narrow fourth-quarter earnings beat. Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), Halliburton (HAL), and Travelers (TRV) are set to report results on Wednesday.

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Alibaba (BABA) Partners with This Nuclear Power Firm to Support AI Growth
data.description:
Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba (BABA) is making a strategic move to lock in long-term power supply as its AI business grows, setting up a new venture with one of China's leading nuclear power companies. The company has formed a 250 million yuan ($35.9 million) joint venture with China National Nuclear Power and other partners, according to corporate filings. However, the details on the exact scope of operations have not been disclosed.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Alibaba has already committed more than $53 billion to AI research and development over the coming years, with a large portion expected to go towards building and expanding data centers. This deal could help the company lock in stable energy as those plans move forward.

Power Is Becoming a Real Constraint

Power supply is becoming a real issue as AI use grows. Large AI data centers need a huge amount of steady electricity, pushing tech companies to look for long-term power solutions.

Globally, companies like Meta Platforms (META) and Microsoft (MSFT) have already signed deals with nuclear power providers to support their data centers.

Governments are also paying closer attention. As AI demand rises, concerns are growing around power supply and electricity prices. In China, government policies have leaned more towards using the country's world-leading solar and wind buildout to meet computing growth. Meanwhile, in the U.S., the Trump administration has been pushing plans that would have technology companies help pay for new power plants, as AI growth puts more strain on the electricity grid.

Is Alibaba Stock a Buy Now?   

Wall Street remains constructive on the stock. Alibaba stock carries a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 unanimous Buy ratings over the past three months. The average BABA price target stands at $203.09, suggesting roughly 25.06% upside from current levels.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: SanDisk Stock Soars to Record High Ahead of Q2 Earnings – Buy Now or Wait?
data.description:
SanDisk stock (SNDK) hit a new all-time high on Tuesday after jumping 9.5%, topping the S&P 500 (SPX). Shares added another 1% in after-hours trading. The latest surge came after five-star-rated analyst Asiya Merchant at Citi raised her price target on SNDK from $280 to $490, maintaining a Buy rating. Even after the stock's sharp run-up, SanDisk remains on Citi's short-term upside watch list ahead of its Q2 earnings report on January 29. Merchant also expects the tight supply and strong demand environment that has driven the stock higher to remain in place through 2027.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That said, Citi noted that other memory and storage stocks could offer better upside, given how sharply SanDisk shares have already climbed over the past year. The firm currently prefers hard disk drive makers such as Western Digital (WDC) and Seagate Technology (STX), which may have more room to run as drive prices increase.

In Q2, Wall Street expects the company to post earnings of $3.32 per share, up sharply from $1.23 a year ago. Meanwhile, revenue is forecast to reach $2.63 billion.

Why Did Citi Raise SanDisk's Price Target to $490?

SNDK's price target upgrade came as part of Citi's 2026 outlook for the technology hardware sector. Merchant said hyperscaler data center spending remains strong, supporting demand across key areas like power, storage, connectors, and fiber. That spending backdrop is good news for companies like SanDisk, which directly benefit from growing demand for data center and enterprise storage. Merchant said the company is still seeing strong, unconstrained demand, with growth running in the mid-20% range.

In addition, Merchant said SanDisk is not changing its supply plans, choosing instead to balance near-term profits with long-term growth goals in an uncertain market.

Is SanDisk a Buy or Sell?

So far this year, SNDK is up about 90%. Even more eye-catching, the stock has surged over 1,100% since spinning off from Western Digital last February, making it one of the market's biggest breakout stories.

Looking ahead, Wall Street analysts rate SanDisk stock as a Moderate Buy, with 11 Buy ratings and four Holds over the past three months. However, the recent rally has pushed the stock well above its average price target of $357.53, suggesting more than 20% downside from current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: GameStop Stock (GME) Surges on CEO Ryan Cohen's Massive Share Purchase
data.description:
Shares of GameStop (GME) rose 3.7% in after-hours trading yesterday after CEO Ryan Cohen bought an additional 500,000 shares, increasing his stake to 9.2%. According to an SEC filing, Cohen purchased the shares in multiple transactions at a weighted average price of $21.12 per share, valued at roughly $10.56 million.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Following this latest purchase, Cohen now holds 41,582,626 shares in GameStop, including approximately 3.7 million warrants. The filing also shows that Cohen has invested about $117.4 million of his own money over time to build the position. The move signals growing confidence in the company's future prospects and boosts investor sentiment.

Cohen Has Skin in GameStop's Game

Cohen first took a 9% stake in GameStop in August 2020, when the video game retailer became a well-known meme stock. The company's board named Cohen President and CEO in September 2023, and he agreed to serve without pay while keeping his chairmanship. This came after Cohen became the largest investor and Chairman in June 2021. GameStop, once famous as a meme stock, has substantially improved its financials and business over the years.  

Early this month, the board awarded Cohen a performance-based stock option for 171.5 million shares at $20.66 per share, potentially reaching $35 billion depending on conditions. The option fully vests if the company reaches a $100 billion market cap and $10 billion in cumulative EBITDA (earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization). This move underscores strong backing for Cohen's leadership.

Is GameStop a Good Stock to Buy Now?

GameStop's market capitalization has risen from roughly $1.3 billion when Cohen joined the board in 2021 to around $9.45 billion currently, marking a gain of over 600%. During the 2021 meme stock frenzy, it peaked at around $34 billion.  

Under Cohen's leadership, GameStop achieved a net income of $421.8 million over the most recent four fiscal quarters, signaling meaningful financial improvement and boosting market confidence in its growth potential. Yet, GME shares have declined 23.2% over the past year.

According to TipRanks A.I. Analyst, GameStop has a Neutral rating of 62 and a price target of $22, implying 4.3% upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Netflix Locks In $8.2B in New Debt for Warner Buyout — Should Investors Worry?
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) has reportedly lined up an additional $8.2 billion in debt, strengthening its all-cash offer for Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) streaming and studio assets. For investors, it's reasonable to worry about higher leverage as Netflix is pulled into an expensive bidding war, hurting NFLX stock in the near term. However, the longer-term picture looks more encouraging.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Meanwhile, Netflix also reported its Q4 FY25 results, beating earnings estimates and showing solid subscriber growth. However, NFLX stock fell nearly 5% in after-hours trading due to soft Q1 guidance and lingering uncertainty around the Warner deal. Yesterday, Netflix switched its Warner offer to an all-cash deal, aiming to block rival Paramount Skydance (PSKY) in the bidding war.

Netflix Secures $8.2B in Debt

Banks, including Wells Fargo & Co., BNP Paribas, and HSBC, have begun selling $8.2 billion in short-term loans to help Netflix fund its planned acquisition. According to reports, the loans are being syndicated to the same banks that provided Netflix's earlier long-term financing. This additional bridge funding allows Netflix to make its bid entirely in cash, instead of the previous mix of cash and stock.

Netflix originally arranged $59 billion in bridge loans to back its bid. It later refinanced around $25 billion with longer-term loans, including two term loans and a revolving credit facility. With the latest syndicated loans, the company now has roughly $42.2 billion in bridge loans to refinance.

Should Investors Worry?

Investors remain concerned that the Warner Bros. deal could push Netflix into areas outside its core business, risking management losing focus. Netflix also said in Tuesday's shareholder letter that it will temporarily pause share buybacks to save cash for the deal, which may have added to investor worries.

Over the long term, if the Warner deal goes through, it could stabilize NFLX stock. Expanding content scale, increasing ad revenue potential, and adding valuable IP like HBO and Warner franchises could boost future cash flow and earnings growth.

Analysts also note that Netflix's fundamentals (subscriber base, ad growth) remain solid, which could support valuation once execution risks fade.

Is Netflix a Good Stock to Buy?

Currently, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Netflix stock based on 24 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sell recommendations. The average NFLX stock price target of $124.80 indicates 43.02% upside potential.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Netflix Stock (NFLX) Slides after Q4 Earnings as Investors Weigh What Comes Next
data.description:
Netflix (NFLX) stock moved over 5% lower in after-hours trading on Tuesday, even after the company reported better-than-expected Q4 results, as investors focused on Netflix's proposed all-cash deal for Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and its broader strategy rather than the quarter itself. The reaction suggests the market is looking past near-term performance and weighing what comes next for the streaming giant.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

To add context, Netflix posted fourth-quarter revenue of $12.1 billion, modestly above Wall Street expectations of $11.97 billion, while adjusted earnings came in at $0.56 per share, also slightly ahead of forecasts of $0.55 per share. Subscriber growth remained strong, with paid memberships climbing to 325 million, reinforcing Netflix's position as the largest paid streaming platform globally.

Despite those positives, the market reaction was cautious.

Investors Look Beyond Recent Results

Investors appeared less focused on Netflix's quarterly results and more on what the company plans to do next. Netflix said it will keep spending heavily on content and new projects, which could weigh on margins in the short term, even if it supports growth over time.

For 2026, Netflix expects revenue between $50.7 billion and $51.7 billion. While that still points to solid growth, the lower end of the range came in slightly below expectations, adding to the cautious reaction.

Ads and Live Content Remain Key Growth Drivers

Advertising is becoming a more important part of Netflix's business. The company said ad revenue more than doubled last year and is expected to roughly double again in 2026 to about $3 billion. Ads give Netflix more ways to make money, but growing this business also brings higher costs.

Netflix is also expanding live content, including sports and events outside the U.S., as it looks to keep viewers engaged beyond traditional shows and movies. To support this effort, the company is building new operations centers in international markets.

The Warner Bros. Deal Raises New Questions

Investors are also watching Netflix's proposed all-cash deal for Warner Bros. Discovery closely. Netflix said it would pause share buybacks to save cash and has secured large bridge loans to help fund the deal.

Management believes the acquisition would add a large and valuable content library and support long-term growth. Still, the size of the deal, along with execution and regulatory risks, has shifted investor focus away from short-term results and toward how well Netflix can manage costs and balance sheet pressure.

Is NFLX Stock a Good Buy Now? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on NFLX stock based on 26 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average NFLX price target of $124.80 per share implies 43% upside potential. However, it's worth noting that estimates will likely change following the earnings report.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
